content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Parse bounded JSON-LD as untrusted data, validate known shapes, preserve unknowns, and avoid eligibility or ranking claims.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Jsonld Admission model · Theory Jsonld Admission, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Jsonld Admission precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Jsonld Admission model · Theory Jsonld Admission, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 1196 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile title, canonical, robots directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and explicit search non-claims; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse bounded JSON-LD as untrusted data, validate known shapes, preserve unknowns, and avoid eligibility or ranking claims.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Jsonld Admission model · Theory Jsonld Admission, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Valid JSON syntax proves structured data meaning and search eligibility.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-jsonld-admission-12l326.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-jsonld-admission-12l326(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_jsonld_admission_12l326\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?metadataSignals < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?explainedSignals < 15 \|\| explainedSignals > metadataSignals)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?conflictCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rankingClaims > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using parse and bounded plus specific case evidence.
